name: opt-next2
author: lordshashank
notes: Supersedes opt-next (all its changes included) with four new optimizations, each A/B-measured on the 2^21 arena task. (1) **arm64 asm gate widened from Apple-only to all aarch64**: the hand-scheduled Montgomery mul/sqr (1.40x field-mul throughput) was dormant on the canonical Neoverse/Cobalt runner for no technical reason - the win comes from breaking clang's serialized __int128 carry chains, which penalizes any wide arm64 core. (2) **Signed-Booth digit windows in the legacy affine Pippenger** (reusing booth_recode.hpp): digits in [-2^(c-1), 2^(c-1)], bucket = |digit|, sign applied branchlessly as points enter buckets; halved bucket range shifts the window optimum one bit wider at equal bucket memory (17 -> 16 rounds at 2^21), bucket reduction work halves; -3.8% MSM-stage in same-binary interleaved A/B; BB_MSM_UNSIGNED=1 reverts at runtime. (3) **Point-prefetch lookahead deepened 32 -> 256** in bucket accumulation: the bucket-sorted schedule makes point loads random across 128 MB, so accumulation is DRAM-latency-bound (~470ns/add vs ~85ns of math); swept 32/64/128/256/512 -> accumulate stage -6.2%. (4) **Memory: prover polynomials released after their last read** (the Gemini rho-batching pass) via an opt-in consume flag mirroring consume_circuit, and the **gate-separator pow table split into two half-domain tables** (Dao-Thaler split-eq: 64 MiB -> ~100 KB, one extra mul per lookup, L1-resident vs DRAM-streamed). Linux-arm64 container A/B (3 interleaved pairs, idle host, same toolchain as CI): baseline 31.91s/1983MiB -> candidate 22.48s/1847MiB = -29.5% time, -6.9% peak RSS. Proofs verify against the pinned baseline VK; ecc_tests pass; claims below are Apple M-series local grade.mjs --runs=5 (advisory). See https://github.com/lordshashank/aztec-packages/pull/3 and RESEARCH.md.
